subject: Support Policy updates between PAP and PDPs
commit_message:
Support Policy updates between PAP and PDPs

Change to send only the policies that need to be updated (deployed/undeployed)
rather than full of policies to DMaap via the PDP_UPDATE message

Issue-ID: POLICY-2112
Change-Id: I65f8bf46f230afb9d4b6937cb89af1d9f05d2fbd
Signed-off-by: waynedunican <wayne.dunican@est.tech>

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

Currently, only one UpdateReq will ever be in the PdpRequests queue for a given PDP.  It does this by replacing the PDP_UPDATE message that's already in the queue with the new PDP_UPDATE request.  Now that PDP_UPDATE messages contain deltas, this becomes a bit tricker.  To accommodate the deltas, one of two approaches must be taken:
1) continue to only put one UpdateReq in the queue.  This requires modification of UpdateReq.reconfigure2() to overlay the full policy list with the new list and to merge the deploy & undeploy lists from the new PDP_UPDATE into the existing PDP_UPDATE
2) allow more than one UpdateReq in the queue at a time.  This is probably the simplest approach, though it opens the door to unbounded growth of the queue.  If this approach is taken, then UpdateReq.reconfigure() must be modified to skip calling reconfigure2() and return false.  In addition, the comment for PdpRequests.requests must be updated to indicate that more than four requests can be in the queue, because multiple UpdateReq objects may be enqueued at a time

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1
> 
> Currently, only one UpdateReq will ever be in the PdpRequests queue for a given PDP.  It does this by replacing the PDP_UPDATE message that's already in the queue with the new PDP_UPDATE request.  Now that PDP_UPDATE messages contain deltas, this becomes a bit tricker.  To accommodate the deltas, one of two approaches must be taken:
> 1) continue to only put one UpdateReq in the queue.  This requires modification of UpdateReq.reconfigure2() to overlay the full policy list with the new list and to merge the deploy & undeploy lists from the new PDP_UPDATE into the existing PDP_UPDATE
> 2) allow more than one UpdateReq in the queue at a time.  This is probably the simplest approach, though it opens the door to unbounded growth of the queue.  If this approach is taken, then UpdateReq.reconfigure() must be modified to skip calling reconfigure2() and return false.  In addition, the comment for PdpRequests.requests must be updated to indicate that more than four requests can be in the queue, because multiple UpdateReq objects may be enqueued at a time

*****************************
I am okay with option 2 but we need to see how it impacts in case of PDP_UPDATE and PDP_STATE_CHANGE pairs. I mean the case where PDP_STATE_CHANGE needs to be followed immediately after a specific PDP_UPDATE is successful. And PDP_STATE_CHANGE is not sent if a specific PDP_UPDATE failed.

Patch Set 7:

> I am okay with option 2 but we need to see how it impacts in case of PDP_UPDATE and PDP_STATE_CHANGE pairs. I mean the case where PDP_STATE_CHANGE needs to be followed immediately after a specific PDP_UPDATE is successful. And PDP_STATE_CHANGE is not sent if a specific PDP_UPDATE failed.

I added some comments to the JIRA for option 1.  Event for option 2, UpdateReq.isSameContent() must be modified to take the additional lists into account.
